$Comment: Round-8b (weapon-pose DERIVED from the real sword model, 2026-07-23): enhance.Custody.Display.Rotation is now derived from Weapon_Sword_Crude's actual blockymodel axes, not guessed. The item references a real Model (Items/Weapons/Sword/Crude.blockymodel), so the bare ItemComponent prop renders that 3D mesh, NOT an icon-extruded flat billboard - there is no diagonal to compensate. Model axes (root frame, at identity): all visible geometry sits under the model's Handle node, whose orientation quaternion (-0.5,-0.5,0.5,0.5) maps Handle-local +X (the pommel-to-tip LONG axis) to model +Y, local +Y (blade WIDTH) to model -Z, and local +Z (blade THICKNESS, i.e. the flat-face normal) to model -X. So at identity the blade LENGTH runs along model +Y (points up), the FLAT-NORMAL is model +/-X (the broad faces point sideways), and the cutting EDGES point along model +/-Z. Engine composition: RpgStations resolveRotationRadians builds Rotation3f(pitch=X, yaw=Y, roll=Z); getQuaternion uses JOML rotationYXZ(Y,X,Z) = Ry(Y)*Rx(X)*Rz(Z), so roll(Z) is applied FIRST in model space, then pitch(X), then yaw(Y). Block facing yaw is ADDED into Y (facing-relative). Target: blade lying flat with its BROAD FACE UP (edge sideways, hilt flat). The math with X=0: Rotation {Y:90, Z:90} gives R = Ry(90)*Rz(90), which maps model +X (flat normal) to world +Y (UP), model +Y (length) to world +Z (block front), model +Z (edge) to world +X (block right) - exactly the target. ROUND-8C IN-GAME UPDATE (2026-07-23): {Y:90,Z:90} CONFIRMED the twist (flat-up, hilt resting) but the blade lay ACROSS the anvil (along block front); current authoring is {Y:0, Z:90}: R = Rz(90) maps flat-normal +X to UP, length +Y to world -X (along the anvil's long axis), edge +Z to +Z. The hilt sits toward block RIGHT (+X, the pommel-anchored render origin); to swap the hilt/tip ends author Rotation.Y:180 and flip Offset.X's sign. VALIDATION against both prior data points: {X:90} alone maps length +Y to world +Z (horizontal, correct) but edge +Z to world -Y (vertical), i.e. the blade lies on its EDGE / edge-up, matching the maintainer's report; {X:90,Z:90} maps length +Y to world -X (a 90-degree SPIN in the horizontal plane vs {X:90}) with the edge still vertical, matching 'spun 90 in the horizontal plane, still edge-up'. Why {X:90,Z:90} failed: roll(Z) applies BEFORE pitch(X), so it spins the still-upright blade in-plane instead of twisting it about its own long axis; twisting via yaw(Y, applied LAST) is the fix, so {Y:90, Z:90} with NO X pitch lays it flat. In THIS derived frame: Z(roll) is the FLAT-vs-EDGE twist knob (Z:90 = flat up, Z:270/-90 = flat down but still lying flat, Z:0/180 = blade stands vertical), and Y(yaw) is the horizontal-DIRECTION knob. Offset changes: the old Offset.X:0.3 (which compensated the {X:90,Z:90} pose whose length pointed along world X) is REMOVED - the length no longer runs along X, so an X shift would only shove the weapon sideways off the anvil ridge. The weapon's render origin sits near the POMMEL, so the mesh extends most of its length toward the tip (now world +Z / block front) and overhangs that edge; with the round-8c yaw (Y:0) the length runs along the block X axis, so the pull-back moved to Offset.X:0.4 (the tip extends toward -X; +0.4 pulls the weapon back toward the anvil-top center along its own length) and the earlier Offset.Z:-0.4 was removed with it. Offset.Y stays 0.55: the new flat pose is vertically THINNER than the old on-edge pose (its lowest point sits about 0.2 block higher at the same offset), which itself counteracts the reported sink, so height is held and made the first nudge. IN-GAME TUNING LADDER for this derived frame (plain JSON leaves, no engine rebuild): (1) blade SPUN the wrong way in the horizontal plane (points across the anvil instead of along it, or vice versa) -> adjust Rotation.Y by +/-90; (2) blade lies on its EDGE / edge-up instead of flat -> confirm NO X pitch leaf is present and keep Z:90; if it lies flat but the wrong broad face is up, flip the twist sign Z:90 <-> Z:270 (i.e. -90); (3) blade standing VERTICAL -> Z drifted to 0/180, set it back to Z:90; (4) sunk INTO or FLOATING above the anvil -> Offset.Y in 0.05 steps; (5) overhangs one END of the anvil (along the blade length) -> Offset.X in 0.1 steps (smaller values slide toward the tip end, larger toward the hilt end); (6) off-center SIDEWAYS -> Offset.Z in 0.1 steps; (7) hilt on the wrong END -> Rotation.Y 0 <-> 180 and flip Offset.X's sign. FALLBACK if the client's item-mesh renderer visually IGNORES pitch/yaw/roll for a bare ItemComponent prop (the one thing server source cannot prove): remove the Rotation leaves (the weapon stays upright, zero regression), keep the schema, and escalate to a lying-flat custom display block or a Blockbench prop model - out of scope here. Round-8 facing-relative context (engine commit cc52fb4): Custody.Display Offset/Rotation are relative to the placed block's own yaw - RpgStations reads getBlockRotationIndex at spawn, rotates the horizontal Offset (X/Z) by it and folds the yaw into Rotation.Y; authored +Z = block front, +X = block right, Y vertical; a default-orientation placement (yaw 0) is the identity so the block frame equals the world frame. convert.Custody.Display (placed ingot, Offset.Y 0.52) and the sawmill's placed log (Offset.Y -0.1) author ONLY a vertical Offset.Y with no horizontal shift and no Rotation, so both the facing-relative change and this weapon-pose change leave them BYTE-IDENTICAL at any orientation - deliberately untouched. Step-synced puppet swings (engine round-8): the enhance ritual's strike1/strike2 steps author a per-step Puppet.Clip of MMO_Emote_Hammer so the skinned puppet visibly HAMMERS on both strike beats, played once at each step's iteration entry; the engine SUPPRESSES the generic engage/swing puppet clip for the whole ritual so there is no double-fire. settle authors no clip (the puppet idles for the settling pause), and stamp keeps its Puppet.Prop.Source None empty-hands override (a Prop but no Clip, so no hammer swing fires on the enchant-flourish beat). The anvil work station (design section 9.5, phase 2 leg E): two diegetic actions selected by held/placed item - Convert (sharpen a vanilla metal bar) and Enhance (the flagship Stamp-step ritual that rolls stats onto a placed weapon). Both gate on holding a hammer (Tool, station-level, shared). Reuses the vanilla Anvil bench model (Common/Blocks/Benches/Anvil.blockymodel) - no new art.
